But this is a huge loophole that luckily no-one pointed out yet.So pilez joined the server after the match and that doesnt count but you added the rule after the match and that now counts?And also no-one acted upon yetSo pilez joined the server after the match and that doesnt count but you added the rule after the match and that now counts?The problem with the whole situation is:
It wasn't clarified in the rules what a registered player is.
However, it has always been acted out in the same way: Player isn't on the team, as per the rules, he's not registered.

I consider this a loophole that was found just now. A major loop-hole, that i needs fixing immediately.

We had loopholes in the past, that were patched post-season aswell.
Back then, in "Match-Times", it didn't say:
"5 registered players have to be available at the start of the match and through all its reset.
If 5 registered players of one team are not present at the agreed time, the following will happen:"

So in that way, people were able to start the match with 5 people, and then continue in a 5v4, 3v3 and so on.
While it was a loophole, it wasn't as abusable, because of the fact that the teams put themself at an active disadvantage.
This one can be abused to the point, where they can just say "he was on our team before he left the server". So, the rule can be made fully obsolote and disregarded. It kinda forced our hand. If we didn't do anything about it, other teams are supposed to be able to do it too. If we did something about it, we had to enforce it here too. You know: Same treatment for everyone, Same decisions. That sentiment is applied during a season.If it was a loophole like the Match-Times thing, i would've said: Okay... you found a loophole, it will be fixed next seasonThis isn't like that whatsoever thoUnlucky again, that it happened against you guys specifically, again... And i do feel sorry about that.
